projects.name: Item-Based Collaborative Filtering Using Mutual Information
projects.description: Developed a recommender system utilizing extensive data from hundreds of users and their preferences, resulting in accurate and personalized movie recommendations. Implemented Collaborative Item-Based Filtering technique. Executed a Python program utilizing the Mutual Information technique.
projects.outcome: Validated the techniques through a 50% reduction in processing time and decreased complexity

projects.name: Comparative Analysis of Patient Readmission Models
projects.description: Conducted an algorithmic comparison to optimize factors influencing patient readmission rates. Identified significant factors impacting patient readmission using multiple regression and Boruta importance. Analyzed various algorithms (Artificial Neural Networks, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest).
projects.outcome: Achieved 100% accuracy, surpassing the performance of existing literature in the field

projects.name: Baccalaureate Thesis: Reliability and Lifetime Estimation of LEDs
projects.description: Devised and executed an analytical methodology to accurately estimate the lifespan of solid-state luminaires. Executed time series modeling using extensive laboratory data in Mathematica. Leveraged Minitab for conducting reliability analysis.
projects.outcome: Presented the thesis paper at the annual IEEE conference (RTEICT 2023) in Bangalore, India, and earned subsequent publication in the journal IEEE Xplore

publications.title: Effect of single and multiple doses of low-level laser therapy on viability and proliferation of stem cells from human exfoliated deciduous teeth (SHED)
